title: Better error when illegal bit select of array
body:
---


Author Name: **Jeremy Bennett** (@jeremybennett)
Original Redmine Issue: 509 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2012-05-08
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

The following code fails with an internal error:

```
module t (clk);

    input clk;

    wire  a[0:0];
    wire  b[0:0];

    assign b = 1'b0;
    assign a = b[0];

endmodule

```

The error message is:

```
%Error: t/t_bitsel_array.v:20: Internal: Unexpected Call
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_bitsel_array.v:20: ../V3AstNodes.h:585: Unexpected Call

```

This appears to be valid SystemVerilog. The problem doesn't occur in real code, but was discovered by accident when I mistyped @b[0:0]@ instead of @[0:0]b@ in the declaration, while investigating issue 508.

Please pull a testcase from https://github.com/jeremybennett/verilator/tree/bitsel-array.
